title: ranunculus praecellans
intro: Ranunculus praecellans, also known as the Western Buttercup, is a species of flowering plant in the Ranunculaceae family that is native to the western coast of North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Ranunculus praecellans include reducing inflammation, improving skin health, and potentially aiding in the treatment of certain infections. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
